data.narrative:
Fire Point is a Series A–stage defense and security company founded by Denys Shtilerman, Iryna Terekh, and Yehor Skalyha, with the Ukrainian Ministry of Defense as an anchor investor. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms—operating in the same sector and stage.

The peer-median method applied across four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $400M. The peer set includes Scale AI ($29.0B), Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), spanning a range from $150M to $29.0B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity, customer concentration, and go-to-market traction within early-stage defense technology. The median of $400M anchors the analysis, as it excludes outliers while capturing central tendency across the cohort.

Triangulation across available methods supports a valuation range of $340M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $400M. Key uncertainties include the extent of Ukrainian MoD revenue concentration, international commercialization pathway clarity, and whether the peer set adequately reflects Fire Point's specific product-market fit or regulatory environment. The analysis is limited to one applicable valuation method; incorporation of revenue multiples or DCF approaches would strengthen conviction, pending disclosure of financial metrics.
